tweets.title:
R to @sidhant: Venezuela readout of Delcy-Hardeep Puri meet:

For Venezuela, India represents a market of vital importance in the hydrocarbons sector. Amid the current dynamics of global energy markets, deepening cooperation with New Delhi — which is emerging as one of the world's fastest-growing economies and largest energy consumers — constitutes a strategic step forward.Collaboration in the oil and gas sector is projected to become a fundamental pillar for strengthening Venezuela’s productive capacities and meeting the energy security needs of the Asian giant.This technical and commercial exchange seeks to capitalize on the complementarities between both nations, enabling the creation of new investment schemes and technological development. India’s economy, characterized by being one of the most rapidly expanding in the current era, features a robust industrial infrastructure and sustained demand for energy resources that aligns perfectly with Venezuela’s export potential.The opening of direct channels of communication and cooperation with the Asian giant offers a pathway for the transfer of technical knowledge, access to diversified markets, and the strengthening of Venezuela’s position in the new multipolar global scenario.
tweets.content:

India’s economy, characterized by being one of the most rapidly expanding in the current era, features a robust industrial infrastructure and sustained demand for energy resources that aligns perfectly with Venezuela’s export potential.The opening of direct channels of communication and cooperation with the Asian giant offers a pathway for the transfer of technical knowledge, access to diversified markets, and the strengthening of Venezuela’s position in the new multipolar global scenario.

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
4th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Israel and Lebanon agreed to a conditional ceasefire following US-mediated talks in Washington. The deal is contingent on a complete halt to hostilities, including all Hezbollah attacks.

● Lebanese reports indicate continued IDF strikes in several southern Lebanon villages despite the announcement.

● Hezbollah mortar fire struck a UNIFIL post near Debin (Dibbine) in southern Lebanon overnight, killing one peacekeeper and wounding two. The IDF traced the launches to the Al Qatrani area.

● The US House of Representatives passed a war powers resolution (215-208) demanding an immediate halt to American military operations against Iran. President Trump dismissed the resolution.

● The IAEA Director General urged Iran to fully cooperate, explain the fate of its enriched uranium stockpiles following last year’s strikes, and resume full inspections.

END OF THE BRIEF

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
3rd Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The IRGC targeted the cargo vessel MSC PANAYA with missiles in retaliation for a reported US strike on an oil tanker in the Strait of Hormuz.

● US forces struck a telecommunications tower south of Qeshm Island.

● The IRGC claimed responsibility for missile and drone attacks targeting US military assets in Kuwait and Bahrain. Most threats were intercepted; Kuwait reported at least one fatality, injuries, and the temporary closure of Kuwait International Airport.

● Kuwait has expelled two Iranian diplomats, declaring them persona non grata.

● The US conducted strikes on Iranian military targets, including a command and ground control site on Qeshm Island near the Strait of Hormuz.

● CENTCOM enforced measures against the Botswana-flagged tanker M/T Lexie as it attempted to reach Kharg Island.

● Iran has frozen all back-channel communications with Washington over Israeli operations in Lebanon, contradicting President Trump’s recent claims that messages from Tehran continue to arrive daily.

END OF THE BRIEF

Manufacturing aircraft is the easy part. (Not so easy in our case). However Supporting 100–200 aircraft for the next 40 years is the hard part.

The real question isn’t whether India can build the Il-114-300 or SJ-100 under licence. It’s whether the MRO, spares, overhaul and supply-chain ecosystem can keep fleet availability high throughout its service life.

Airlines buy support as much as they buy aircraft. If an airline has an aircraft grounded in Chennai, Delhi, or Jakarta, can parts arrive within hours rather than weeks?

BREAKING: Ministry of Defence has officially implemented the Delegation of Financial Powers to Defence Services (DFPDS-2026). This major policy overhaul grants enhanced financial authority for revenue-related procurement, decentralising a budget value exceeding ₹1.25 Lakh Crore annually.

- Operational Readiness: Grants field commanders direct financial autonomy to expedite decision-making and enhance immediate combat preparedness.

- Doubled Works Capital: Financial allocations for structural and infrastructure works projects are officially doubled to eliminate delays and ensure timely completion.

- Indigenisation & Innovation: Significantly expands financial ceilings for research and development to reduce reliance on foreign OEMs. This directly boosts domestic private players, including MSMEs and defense startups.

This marks a structural shift toward military decentralisation and self-reliance in defense manufacturing.

tweets.title:
Senior Pakistani politician Qamar Zaman Kaira has openly questioned the 12 refugee seats in PoJK, arguing that they undermine the rights of PoJK residents.

Under the current system, refugees from Jammu & Kashmir living in Pakistan elect 12 representatives to the PoJK Legislative Assembly despite residing outside the territory. These legislators can vote on the formation of the PoJK government.

Think of it like residents of one city being allowed to elect councillors who can decide the mayor of another city where they do not even live. The local population may vote, but the final outcome can still be shaped by people outside the territory.

This highlights how PoJK residents do not fully control their own assembly, as through refugee constituencies, the Pakistani establishment can influence government formation.

This mechanism provides Islamabad a strategic leverage over PoJK.
